title: A Conclusion, Drawn From The Epigrams, Epigram On The Busts, And Sent To The Drapier
content:
Since Anna, whose bounty thy merits had fed,
    Ere her own was laid low, had exalted thy head:
    And since our good queen to the wise is so just,
    To raise heads for such as are humbled in dust,
    I wonder, good man, that you are not envaulted;
    Prithee go, and be dead, and be doubly exalted.
meaning:
Jonathan Swift's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "A Conclusion, Drawn From The Epigrams, Epigram On The Busts, And Sent To The Drapier"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
